verses.text: Receive the weak in faith; 'let not him that eateth despise him that eateth not.' Mutual acceptance. God has received both; so must we.
Receive the weak in faith; 'let not him that eateth despise him that eateth not.' Mutual acceptance. God has received both; so must we.
Receive the weak in faith; 'let not him that eateth despise him that eateth not.' Mutual acceptance. God has received both; so must we.
Who art thou that judgest another's servant? 'let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.' To his own Master. Each answers to the Lord, not to us.
Who art thou that judgest another's servant? 'let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.' To his own Master. Each answers to the Lord, not to us.
Who art thou that judgest another's servant? 'let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.' To his own Master. Each answers to the Lord, not to us.
None lives or dies to himself; 'whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord's.' Wholly His. We belong to Christ in life and death.
None lives or dies to himself; 'whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord's.' Wholly His. We belong to Christ in life and death.
None lives or dies to himself; 'whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord's.' Wholly His. We belong to Christ in life and death.
Why judge thy brother? 'we shall all stand before the judgment seat'; each shall give account. One Judge. We leave the judging to God.
Why judge thy brother? 'we shall all stand before the judgment seat'; each shall give account. One Judge. We leave the judging to God.
Why judge thy brother? 'we shall all stand before the judgment seat'; each shall give account. One Judge. We leave the judging to God.
Judge rather that none put 'a stumblingblock... in his brother's way.' Care for the weak. Liberty must not wound a brother's conscience.
Judge rather that none put 'a stumblingblock... in his brother's way.' Care for the weak. Liberty must not wound a brother's conscience.
Judge rather that none put 'a stumblingblock... in his brother's way.' Care for the weak. Liberty must not wound a brother's conscience.
'The kingdom of God is... righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.' Inward kingdom. Religion is not in meat and drink but in the heart.
'The kingdom of God is... righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.' Inward kingdom. Religion is not in meat and drink but in the heart.
'The kingdom of God is... righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.' Inward kingdom. Religion is not in meat and drink but in the heart.
Follow the things 'which make for peace'; do nothing to make a brother stumble. Peace and care. Love yields its liberty for another's good.
Follow the things 'which make for peace'; do nothing to make a brother stumble. Peace and care. Love yields its liberty for another's good.
Follow the things 'which make for peace'; do nothing to make a brother stumble. Peace and care. Love yields its liberty for another's good.
Happy is he that condemns not himself; 'whatsoever is not of faith is sin.' Act in faith. To do what conscience doubts is sin.
